page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.medium is an arm64-based compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical core operating at 2.8 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of system memory. The processor architecture includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, alongside hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the single-core architecture of the c8gn.medium delivers strong single-threaded performance in specific workloads, such as gzip compression at 29.99 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.17 GB/sec. However, due to its single-vCPU configuration, multi-core and parallelized benchmarks are limited, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores falling into lower performance tiers. Memory bandwidth tests show 115.7 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 30.4 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, while database operations and Redis SET performance reflect its entry-level resource footprint.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8gn.medium offers a low-cost entry point into the Graviton8 ARMv9 architecture, providing dedicated compute resources without the overhead of multi-core configurations. This makes it highly resource-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded tasks. Appropriate workloads include basic web serving, lightweight single-threaded utility scripts, microservices, and development or testing environments that benefit from ARM64 architecture compatibility.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.medium is an entry-level, compute-optimized virtual machine utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and ARM64 architecture. It is equipped with a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 core running at 2.8 GHz, 2.0 GB of RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU. Performance benchmarks show strong single-threaded capabilities, notably in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are constrained by the single-core design. This instance provides a cost-effective entry point for lightweight ARM64 workloads, making it suitable for microservices, single-threaded utility tasks, and basic web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.medium is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring one dedicated 2.8 GHz Graviton Neoverse-V2 core, 2.0 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.medium is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring one dedicated 2.8 GHz Graviton core, 2.0 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A single-core ARM64 compute instance offering dedicated Graviton performance and baseline networking for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: Single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 core running at 2.8 GHz
2.0 GB of system memory with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Strong single-threaded performance in compression and decompression benchmarks
Designed for lightweight microservices, utility scripts, and ARM64 development
